designDNA.concept: Festive Gujarati elegance — a warm, luminous celebration of culture and love expressed through cream-and-gold minimalism, poetic typography, and subtle sacred motifs. The design balances spiritual reverence (Ganesh invocation, ॐ symbol) with modern restraint, creating a wedding identity that feels both ancestrally rooted and contemporarily refined.
designDNA.tone: Festive, warm, and spiritually grounded — celebratory without being loud, elegant without being cold. Carries the intimacy of a family blessing and the joy of a grand Gujarati celebration simultaneously.
designDNA.community: Modern non-religious Gujarati-heritage couple (Kavya & Mihir) — culturally expressive, aesthetically minimal, English-language audience blending diaspora sensibility with traditional ceremony pride.
designDNA.styleDirection: Minimal elegant with festive Gujarati cultural accents. Clean spatial hierarchy, warm cream base, restrained ornamentation using gold line-work, sacred typography, and ambient glow effects rather than heavy pattern or illustration.
designDNA.textureStyle: Warm cream base with a subtle radial dot-grid overlay (gold and maroon at very low opacity). Ambient radial glow blobs in gold (top-left) and maroon (bottom-right) create soft depth. Frosted glass surfaces use backdrop-filter blur with semi-transparent cream-to-gold-pale gradients. No photographic textures — all atmosphere achieved through CSS gradients and opacity layering.
designDNA.shadowStyle: Soft, warm, and color-tinted. Shadows use maroon or gold hues rather than neutral grey — e.g. rgba(123,30,53,0.35) for maroon-tinted depth and rgba(201,151,58,0.15) for gold-tinted lift. Inset highlights (inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.8)) simulate surface luminosity. Drop shadows are diffuse and generous (24–32px blur), never harsh.

preferredAnimations: slow radial glow pulse (6–7s ease-in-out alternate)
floating petal drift with opacity fade (8s)
gentle vertical float for icons (3s ease-in-out alternate)
ॐ symbol text-shadow glow breathe (4s alternate)

designDNA.layoutPersonality: Three-column grid with dominant center and narrow decorative side pillars. Strong vertical rhythm with generous gap spacing (24–28px between content blocks). Centered text alignment in the focal zone. Ornamental top and bottom bands frame the section. Collapses to single-column on mobile with side columns hidden. Pill and strip containers used for grouped metadata (event details). Typography scale is dramatic — oversized staggered display names contrast with micro-uppercase labels.
sectionRules: Maintain the warm cream (#FAF5EC) background as the base — never go white or dark
Always use the maroon-to-gold gradient for primary display text or headline accents
Use Rozha One only for couple names or major display headings — never for body copy
Use Cormorant Garamond italic for poetic, invitation-style prose passages
Use Manrope for all UI labels, micro-caps, buttons, and metadata
Preserve the ॐ / Ganesh invocation as a recurring sacred anchor element near section tops
Use ◆ diamond glyphs and thin gold rule lines as separators — never heavy borders
Keep all decorative motifs (petals, ornaments) at aria-hidden=true and CSS-only
Apply frosted glass pill treatment (backdrop-filter + semi-transparent cream gradient + gold border) for grouped info containers
Shadow colors must always be tinted maroon or gold — never neutral grey
Animations must remain subtle and slow (≥3s duration) — no bouncy or attention-grabbing motion
Micro-uppercase labels must always use letter-spacing ≥ 0.2em and font-weight 600–700
Side decorative columns (dot-line-diamond) may reappear as subtle margin ornaments in wider layouts
Do not introduce new typefaces — only Rozha One, Cormorant Garamond, and Manrope
Do not use photographic backgrounds, dark backdrops, or neon/cool-tone colors
Do not use heavy drop shadows with neutral black — always warm-tinted
Do not use square or hard-cornered cards — use pill badges or softly rounded surfaces (16px+) for containers
Emoji cultural icons (🪔🌼🎶) may recur as lightweight decorative accents in event or schedule sections
Bottom and top ornamental bands with gold gradient lines should bookend major sections for visual continuity
Maintain festive but restrained energy — every decorative element must earn its place

cssVariables.--gradient-band: linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, rgba(201,151,58,0.12) 30%, rgba(201,151,58,0.18) 50%, rgba(201,151,58,0.12) 70%, transparent)
